perturbation.original_text: 4.5. PAYMENT TERMS. Impresse shall pay the fees set forth in Sections 4.2 [SLOTTING FEES], 4.3 [BANNER/NEWSLETTER PURCHASE COMMITMENT] and 4.4 [HOSTING FEE] as an aggregate, totaling $[*] payable in four equal quarterly installments of $[*] beginning on May 31, 2000 and thereafter on August 31, 2000 November 30, 2000 and February 28, 2001, respectively.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.5. PAYMENT TERMS. Impresse shall pay the fees set forth in Sections 4.2 [SLOTTING FEES], 4.3 [BANNER/NEWSLETTER PURCHASE COMMITMENT] and 4.4 [HOSTING FEE] as an aggregate, totaling $[*] payable in four equal quarterly installments of $[*] beginning on May 31, 2000, with subsequent installments due 90 days after the prior payment. Impresse also has the option to defer payments with prior notice and a 5% penalty for each deferred installment.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the payment schedule for the fees. The original clause specifies fixed quarterly dates (May 31, August 31, November 30, and February 28), while the modified clause stipulates payments due every 90 days from the first payment on May 31. Also, the introduction of the 'defer payments' clause with a 5% penalty introduces uncertainty and contradicts the fixed payment terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.5. PAYMENT TERMS. Impresse shall pay the fees set forth in Sections 4.2 [SLOTTING FEES], 4.3 [BANNER/NEWSLETTER PURCHASE COMMITMENT] and 4.4 [HOSTING FEE] as an aggregate, totaling $[*] payable in four equal quarterly installments of $[*] beginning on May 31, 2000, with subsequent installments due 90 days after the prior payment. Impresse also has the option to defer payments with prior notice and a 5% penalty for each deferred installment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by altering the payment schedule. The original text specifies fixed quarterly dates, while the modified clause stipulates payments due every 90 days from the first payment on May 31 and introduces an option to defer payments with a 5% penalty. This directly contradicts the fixed payment terms implied in the original version of section 4.5, making it unclear which payment schedule should be followed. This is a strong inconsistency as per the definition.

perturbation.original_text: 10.3. NO ASSIGNMENT. Except as otherwise set forth herein, neither party shall transfer, assign or cede any rights or delegate any obligations hereunder, in whole or in part, whether voluntarily or by operation of law, without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent may be withheld at the other party's reasonable business discretion; provided, however, that either party may transfer this Agreement without prior written consent of the other to an Affiliate of such party, or to the surviving party in a merger or consolidation, or to a purchaser of all or substantially all of its assets.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.3. NO ASSIGNMENT. Except as otherwise set forth herein, neither party shall transfer, assign or cede any rights or delegate any obligations hereunder, in whole or in part, whether voluntarily or by operation of law, without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent may be withheld at the other party's sole discretion; provided, however, that either party may transfer this Agreement without prior written consent of the other to an Affiliate of such party, or to the surviving party in a merger or consolidation, or to a purchaser of a controlling interest of its assets.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the conditions for assignment without consent. The original allows consent to be withheld at the other party's 'reasonable business discretion,' implying an objective standard. The modified version grants consent may be withheld at 'sole discretion,' allowing for arbitrary denial. Further, the phrase 'all or substantially all of its assets' is replaced with 'controlling interest of its assets' is more ambiguous as it is unclear what constitutes a 'controlling interest', potentially conflicting with the original intent and creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.3. NO ASSIGNMENT. Except as otherwise set forth herein, neither party shall transfer, assign or cede any rights or delegate any obligations hereunder, in whole or in part, whether voluntarily or by operation of law, without the prior written consent of the other party, which consent may be withheld at the other party's sole discretion; provided, however, that either party may transfer this Agreement without prior written consent of the other to an Affiliate of such party, or to the surviving party in a merger or consolidation, or to a purchaser of a controlling interest of its assets.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates an in-text contradiction. The original text in section 10.3 states that consent to assignment may be withheld at the other party's 'reasonable business discretion,' while the changed text in the same section states that consent may be withheld at 'sole discretion.' This directly contradicts the earlier clause by removing the 'reasonable business discretion' standard, thus giving the other party arbitrary power, which is a strong contradiction. Also, the phrase 'all or substantially all of its assets' is replaced with 'controlling interest of its assets'. It is unclear what constitutes a 'controlling interest', potentially conflicting with the original intent and creating uncertainty. The contradiction is strong and creates uncertainty regarding the standard for withholding consent, thus making enforcement unclear.
